title: The Enemies
content:
The angry wind
    That cursed at me
    Was nothing but an evil sprite
    Vexed with any man's delight.

    And strange it seemed
    That a dark wind
    Should run down from a mountain steep
    And shout as though the world were asleep.

    But when he ceased
    And silence was--
    Who could but fear what evil sprite
    Crept through the tunnels of the night?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Frederick Freeman delivers a powerful performance in "The Enemies"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
